key_insight: ClinRAG-GRAPH combines multimodal graph learning, domain-adversarial robustness, and LLM-based retrieval to improve pre-treatment prediction of breast pCR after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
clinical_relevance: If validated further, this approach could help stratify breast cancer patients before treatment and support more personalized NAC decisions.
method: Builds an intra-patient clinical-prior graph from DCE-MRI, clinical variables, and biopsy biomarkers
Uses a prior-guided relation-aware graph convolutional network for multimodal representation learning
Adds dual-branch domain-adversarial learning and LLM-driven subgraph retrieval for robustness and interpretability
evidence_signal: In a multicenter cohort from two public sources and three in-house centers, the model achieved AUC 0.815 internally and 0.774/0.712 on two external test sets.
watch_item: External performance is lower than internal performance, so real-world deployment would still need prospective validation and careful handling of imaging protocol differences.
paper_title: ClinRAG-GRAPH: Clinical-prior Retrieval-Augmented Graph Model with Domain Adversarial Learning for Breast pCR Prediction
